title: Compliance Checklist — BC rent increase
preconditions: Move-in date or tenancy start date is confirmed.
Confirm scenario description appears brief; additional facts may improve the analysis.
Verify the proposed new rent amount.
Check whether the property qualifies for any exemptions.
steps: Identify the current rent amount and the proposed new rent amount.
Calculate the percentage increase from the current rent.
Verify that written notice was provided at least 90 days in advance.
Confirm that the required 12-month interval between increases has elapsed.
Check whether the property qualifies for any exemptions (new construction, subsidized housing).
Compare the scenario facts against the structured rule limits.
documents: Previous rent amount or last rent receipt
datesToCheck: Required notice period before increase can take effect
Frequency limit — date of last increase
Effective date of the current rule version
uncertainties: The tenancy start date is not specified, which may affect frequency limits.
warnings: This rule has an expired effective date. Verify the current rule before proceeding.
This rule may be outdated. Check for more recent amendments.
